verifiedSignals: Triplication confirmed at src/core/generator.ts lines 531-585, 800-856, 998-1052 (function, method, ctor call-prelude blocks identical modulo indent and label).
Six as-any casts confirmed at lines 555, 573, 824, 844, 1022, 1040 matching REVIEW.md B3.
Per-site differences are only indent, error label, and terminal RPC at line 588 call, 894 callMethod, 1107 instantiate.
kwargsType, renderPositionalParam and overload loops also triplicated at 368-409 and 435-492, 727-798, 929-991.
ts-fx flagged generator.ts as god-file; its duplicate scan missed the triplication, verified manually as instructed.
test/generated_snapshot.test.ts exists but only asserts substrings via toContain, insufficient to catch whitespace regressions.
verdict: Worth doing, scoped to the emit-pipeline extraction plus a minimal file split, not a full rewrite. The triplication is real and confirmed: the call-prelude, guard, and varargs logic is copy-pasted across three sites that differ ONLY in indentation, the error-label literal, and the final RPC call expression, about 210 lines whose only meaningful difference is three string parameters. Centralizing into a descriptor-driven emitter kills all 6 as-any casts in one helper, single-sources the keyword and positional-trim logic, and shrinks the file by about a third. Risk: this is string-template codegen with subtle whitespace contracts, so the refactor MUST be gated by a full-output characterization snapshot taken before any structural change; existing tests assert substrings, not full output, and will not catch a whitespace regression. REVIEW.md labels B3 large; the actual delta is medium because the three copies are mechanically identical, not semantically divergent. Do not chase the file split for its own sake; one new module emit-call.ts falls out naturally. Defer the ts-decl and runtime-shim split.
falsePositives: REVIEW.md B3 rates this large effort; after reading source I downgrade to medium because the three copies are mechanically identical, a near-pure code move not a semantic untangle.

The as-any casts are not a type hole in generator.ts itself; they live inside emitted template-literal strings, affecting the generated wrapper not generator.ts type checking, and were never flagged by tsc. Centralizing is still worth it. This nuance is absent from REVIEW.md.

SIGNAL-DIGEST lists generator.ts as a god-file, but its functions are not in the ts-fx top-cognitive-complexity table (codec.ts, analyzer.ts, tywrap.ts, annotation-parser.ts dominate). generator.ts pain is duplication and file length 1291 lines, not per-function complexity; eliminate-duplication is the right lens.
proposal: Introduce a normalized CallDescriptor and a single indent-parameterized emit pipeline that all three sites consume. New module src/core/emit-call.ts, imported only by generator.ts and not re-exported from index.ts. CallSiteShape carries the only true per-site variance: indent (two spaces for func, four for method and ctor) and errorLabel (the Python callable name used in emitted throw messages). CallDescriptor holds the fields computed identically at all three sites today: positionalParams, varArgsParam, needsKwargsParam, needsVarArgsArray, requiredPosCount, keywordOnlyNames, requiredKwOnlyNames, positionalOnlyNames, hasVarKwArgs, kwargsType, positionalArgExprs. buildCallDescriptor replaces the three hand-inlined computations at lines 349-409, 715-748, 917-950. emitCallPrelude(d, shape) replaces the three callPreludeLines blocks at 531-585, 800-856, 998-1052; indent and throw label come from shape. The six as-any casts collapse to one: the emitted kwargs assignment is written exactly once and can become as Record of string to unknown. emitArgGuards(d, shape) replaces the three guard blocks at 496-525, 859-890, 1054-1085. emitOverloads folds the triplicated overload-family loops at 439-492, 774-797, 967-990 (optional second step). emitCallExpression(kind, target) is the one place the terminal RPC differs, returning the call, callMethod-with-handle, or instantiate verb, kept explicit rather than over-abstracted. generator.ts becomes a thin orchestrator: each site builds a CallDescriptor plus CallSiteShape and concatenates emitCallPrelude, emitArgGuards, site-specific signature and return wrapping, and emitCallExpression. About 600 lines of triplicated emission collapse to one 200-line emit-call.ts plus three 30-line call sites. File split is minimal: generator.ts keeps orchestration (generateModule, generateModuleDefinition, the typed_dict, namedtuple, protocol, dataclass branches, and escapeIdentifier, typeToTs, generic-context helpers); emit-call.ts holds the descriptor and emit helpers. I decline the proposed ts-decl and runtime-shim three-way split: declaration emission at 594-599, 1116-1121, 1188-1192 is small and tightly coupled to the same descriptors, so splitting buys a third file with import churn and no duplication removed. Revisit only if generator.ts is still over 900 lines after emit-call.ts lands.
breakingChanges: Emitted-output change (intended): the six as-any casts in generated wrappers become as Record of string to unknown, changing the byte content of every generated file with optional-positional-plus-kwargs or varargs-plus-kwargs ambiguity. Runtime-neutral but changes snapshots and pinned generated output. If strict byte-preservation is preferred, keep as-any in the helper and defer the cast change to a separate labeled commit.
Internal API: new src/core/emit-call.ts module; no external importers of the inline helpers existed, so no public break, and it is intentionally not added to the index.ts barrel.

Potential whitespace normalization: the per-block conditional-join newline idiom must remain byte-identical; any output shift is a bug enforced against by the characterization snapshot, not an intended break.
verificationPlan: Behavior-preservation is proven by a full-output characterization snapshot taken before any structural edit. Add a vitest toMatchSnapshot suite driving CodeGenerator directly over a fixture matrix covering every branch the three sites exercise: plain positional fn; fn with optional-tail positionals (trim-while loop plus the __candidate as-any path at line 555); fn with star-args only; fn with star-args plus kwargs (needsVarArgsArray plus the varargs as-any path at line 573); fn with required keyword-only params; fn with positional-only params; a class with init exercising the ctor site 998-1052; a class method exercising the method site 800-856; typed_dict, namedtuple, protocol, dataclass classes as untouched regression anchors. Add a generate-level snapshot over the living-app config (living_app.app with stdlib, numpy, pandas, pydantic presets) snapshotting full math.generated.ts plus a stdlib module, extending test/generated_snapshot.test.ts from substring to full content. Commit snapshots on unrefactored code as the golden baseline, then refactor and re-run: snapshots must be byte-identical except the deliberate as-any to as-Record change, reviewed as one intentional diff and re-blessed. Run npm run check:all and re-run ts-fx layout and duplicates to confirm generator.ts drops below the god-file threshold and no new duplication appears. npm run typecheck proves emit-call.ts leaks no any into the generator own types.
steps: Add test/generator_emit_snapshot.test.ts with a fixture matrix (PythonFunction and PythonClass) covering all verification-plan branches, asserting code.typescript and code.declaration via toMatchSnapshot. Run against unmodified generator.ts and commit the snap as the golden baseline; do not edit generator.ts in this step.
Promote test/generated_snapshot.test.ts (or add a sibling) to capture full file content for math plus one stdlib module via generate, not just substrings.

Create src/core/emit-call.ts. Define CallDescriptor and CallSiteShape. Implement buildCallDescriptor by lifting the field computations from generateFunctionWrapper lines 349-409 verbatim; reconcile module-var vs closure forms into one signature.

Implement emitCallPrelude(d, shape) by lifting the 531-585 block, parameterizing the two-space indent with shape.indent and the throw label with shape.errorLabel. Write the kwargs cast exactly once; keep it as-any for now to preserve bytes.
Implement emitArgGuards(d, shape) by lifting 496-525 with the same indent and label parameterization.
Implement emitCallExpression(kind, target) returning the three RPC verbs: call, callMethod with handle, instantiate.

Rewrite generateFunctionWrapper to build a descriptor plus shape (two-space indent, errorLabel func.name) and consume the helpers. Run the characterization suite; function-site snapshots must be byte-identical.
Rewrite the method loop inside generateClassWrapper to consume the helpers with four-space indent and errorLabel method.name. Re-run suite; method snapshots byte-identical.
Rewrite ctorSpec to consume the helpers with four-space indent and errorLabel __init__. Re-run suite; ctor snapshots byte-identical. All six as-any now live in exactly one source location.

Optional separate commit: fold the triplicated kwargsType, renderPositionalParam, and overload-family loops at 368-409 and 435-492, 727-798, 929-991 into buildCallDescriptor plus emitOverloads. Re-run suite; byte-identical.
Deliberate breaking commit, clearly labeled: change the single emitted cast from as-any to as Record of string to unknown. Re-bless affected snapshots; confirm the diff is only those cast sites.

Run npm run check:all and npm run typecheck; re-scan with ts-fx layout and duplicates to confirm generator.ts is below the god-file threshold and the triplication is gone. Update CLAUDE.md repo-structure note to mention core/emit-call.ts.
